titleStmt.title: SII 41.318: original edition by S. Rajagopal
No. 318. (A.R. No. 534 of 1905.) Tiruveḷḷaṟai, Tiruchirappalli Taluk, Tiruchirappalli District. On the second pillar in the rock-cut cave in Pundarikāsha-Perumāḷ Temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai.

p.hi: Chōḻa, Parakēsarivarmaṉ (Uttama-chōḻa ?)., Year 8 = c. 10th Century A.D.

summary.p: It registers a gift of 20 kaḻañju of gold made by Irāyiravan Dēvi Ammanār the wife of ‘the lord who died on elephant-back’ i.e. Prince Rājāditya, for providing for special offerings with 4 nāḻi of rice on the days of two uvā (New Moon and Full Moon days), Sankrānti and Ashṭami, to god Śri Krishna and goddess Śri-Rukmini in the big temple at Tiruveḷḷaṟai, and another gift of gold for a lamp. This is said to be the earliest reference to the worship of Krishna and Rukmini found so for in South Indian Inscriptions.
[Published in SII Vol. III No. 132 and noted in SII Vol. XIX No. 196.]

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kopparakesari panmaṟku yāṇṭu 8 Āvatu tiruveḷḷaṟai periyaśrī
koyilil śrīkṛṣṇarkkum śrīrukminip pirāṭṭiyārkkum Uṭaiyār Āṉaimeṟṟu
ñciṉār deviyār Irāyiraṉṟeviyammaṉār kuṭuttapaṭi Eḻutiyatu śrīkṛṣṇarkku
Iraṇṭuvāvum Aṣṭamiyum saṅgrān=tiyum Innāḷ ḷoṉṟukku nāṉāḻi nāṉāḻi Arici
yāl Amutupaṭiyamutu ceyvikka vaitta poṉ tiruveḷḷaṟaik kallāl mutala
rupatiṉ kaḻañcum viḷakkoṉṟukku vaitta poṉ tiruveḷḷaṟaik kallālThe record stops here.
Digital edition of SII 41.318 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
